name: Chicken Marsala
method: Wash and pat chicken dry, on a plastic lined cutting board pound 1/4 inch thin. In a small bowl combine flour, salt, and pepper. Coat chicken on both sides and set aside. In a large frying pan on medium heat, brown chicken on both sides and remove from pan. Set aside to drain on paper towels. In the same frying pan, add 2 T olive oil, 2 T butter, mushrooms, and saute. Add shallots, garlic, 1 cup of chicken broth, Marsala, 1 t salt, and 1 t pepper. It's best not to oversalt because you can always add more later!

Simmer on low heat, for 20 minutes, then add heavy cream, chicken breasts that you fried earlier and simmer an additional 5 minutes. Sauce will be thick but just in case it's not thick enough you can dissolve 1 T cornstarch or flour in 2 T cold water and add it to the cream slowly. Garnish with parsley. Serving suggestion: Roasted vegetables and bruschetta. Serves 2- 3. Olé This is one of Leo's favorite recipes!
